title: lepidium cartilagineum subsp. crenatifolium
intro: Lepidium cartilagineum subsp. crenatifolium is a subspecies of the Lepidium cartilagineum plant, commonly known as a type of cress or mustard plant, which belongs to the Brassicaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Lepidium cartilagineum subsp. crenatifolium can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine as a poultice or topical application.
